scope: Search-driven lead magnet opportunities for home sauna buyers — research-to-purchase phase

executiveSummary.topFinding: The sauna quiz/configurator is table stakes — Almost Heaven, Nordica, Peak Saunas, MySaunaWorld, Topture, Finnish Sauna Builders, and Airpuria all have one. The gap is not the format; it is the quality and the keyword targeting behind it. No competitor has built a quiz that ranks organically. They are all on-site tools with no SEO footprint. That is the opportunity: build a configurator page that targets high-intent query clusters directly, not just a conversion tool buried on /pages/quiz.
executiveSummary.keyGap: Zero competitors are capturing emails via a downloadable PDF or gated guide. SelectSaunas uses a 5% coupon code exchange. CedarBrook uses a custom design quote form. HavenOfHeat uses a consultation form. The 'home sauna buying guide PDF' and 'home sauna checklist PDF' keyword clusters are almost entirely uncontested as lead magnets. There is no sauna-specific SafetyCulture-quality interactive checklist that ranks.
executiveSummary.immediateWin: Create a standalone page at /tools/home-sauna-finder (or /guides/find-your-sauna) that hosts the configurator quiz AND targets the 'which home sauna is right for me' and 'home sauna buying checklist' keyword clusters. This page does not exist from any content-first sauna brand. Currently the quiz pages that exist are all at /pages/sauna-quiz on Shopify stores — dead for SEO.
executiveSummary.revenueAlignment: Buyer-stage keywords have CPC values of $2-8, indicating strong affiliate and commercial intent. Every email captured from these keywords represents a buyer in a 90-180 day research cycle with a $3K-$15K purchase decision.

buyerStageKeywords.note: Estimated volumes based on industry benchmarks and SERP competition density. Verified directional — not from a paid tool.

tier3_questionBased.intent: buyer wants a structured pre-purchase checklist

competitorLeadMagnetAudit.note: Direct audit of how each major competitor captures emails. Research conducted March 2026.

competitors.emailCapture: Newsletter subscription CTA present. No dedicated lead magnet for buyers.
competitors.leadMagnetGap: No free downloadable resource for buyers. No interactive tool. Purely editorial + paid products.
competitors.opportunity: sauna.guide can own the 'free buyer decision tool' territory they do not cover

competitors.emailCapture: Multi-step 'Custom Sauna Design and Quote' form — name, phone, email, address, file upload. High friction but very qualified when completed.
competitors.leadMagnetGap: No lighter-weight lead magnet before the high-commitment quote form. Buyers who are not ready to talk to a salesperson bounce without capturing their email.
competitors.opportunity: A lower-friction mid-funnel capture (checklist or configurator) would intercept the buyers CedarBrook is losing before they get to quote stage

competitors.emailCapture: Shopify email popup (discount-based, no specific sauna lead magnet). Has a 'Perfect Sauna Quiz' but it is not gated — no email required. Built on Shopify quiz functionality, not content-SEO.
competitors.leadMagnetGap: Quiz results are NOT gated. They recommend products but do not capture email. This is a significant missed conversion. No downloadable guide.
competitors.opportunity: Gate the results with 'Email me this recommendation' and sauna.guide would capture leads Almost Heaven is letting walk away

competitors.emailCapture: Custom Sauna Design and Quote form (very high friction). Blog-only lead capture via newsletter.
competitors.leadMagnetGap: No interactive tool. No quiz. No downloadable guide. All conversion is form-based.
competitors.opportunity: Interactive tools and lighter-friction email capture would differentiate from their high-friction approach

competitors.emailCapture: Hustle popup with email capture. Interactive calculators embedded in content (session duration, heater size, calorie burn) — but calculators are ungated.
competitors.leadMagnetGap: Calculators do not capture email. No downloadable guide. Good content but weak email capture mechanics.
competitors.opportunity: Gating any one of those calculators with a 'email me my results' option would convert their calculator traffic to email list

competitors.emailCapture: Product recommendation quiz (Nordica Sauna Recommendation Quiz — product-specific, goes to their shop).
competitors.leadMagnetGap: All tools guide toward their own products. No neutral buyer education resource. No downloadable guide.
competitors.opportunity: sauna.guide's brand-agnostic positioning is a direct advantage — buyers trust neutral sources over vendor tools

competitors.emailCapture: Newsletter popup offering 5% coupon code. Has a published 'buying checklist' article (ungated).
competitors.leadMagnetGap: The buying checklist article is their best content but it is ungated and does not capture email. Coupon code capture attracts discount buyers, not high-ticket research buyers.
competitors.opportunity: sauna.guide's equivalent checklist page should be gated as a PDF download to capture the research-phase buyer SelectSaunas is giving away for free

competitors.emailCapture: None detected on their cost calculator.
tools: Sauna Cost Calculator (ungated, no email required)
competitors.leadMagnetGap: The most useful sauna tool online and it captures zero emails. Their calculator takes room dimensions, heater type, wood type, and installation method and outputs an estimated cost. No email gate.
competitors.opportunity: Build a similar or better calculator and add an email gate: 'Email me this estimate + a materials list'

competitorMatrixSummary.keyInsight: ZERO competitors gate their quiz with email. ZERO competitors gate their calculator with email. ZERO competitors offer a downloadable PDF guide as a lead magnet. The entire category is leaving conversion on the table. sauna.guide has a clear first-mover opportunity to own gated buyer tools.

existingToolsThatRank.rankingFor: sauna cost calculator, sauna build cost estimator
existingToolsThatRank.whatMakesItRank: Dedicated calculator page, keyword-matched URL, functional tool that answers a clear query
existingToolsThatRank.howToOutrank: Build equivalent at /tools/sauna-cost-calculator, add email gate ('Email me this estimate + a recommended materials list'), add more inputs (indoor vs outdoor, pre-built vs kit vs DIY, state for permit cost estimates)

existingToolsThatRank.rankingFor: sauna heater size calculator, sauna heater sizing
existingToolsThatRank.whatMakesItRank: Multiple competing versions exist — this keyword cluster has 4-5 strong tools already. Lower opportunity.

existingToolsThatRank.rankingFor: sauna lumber calculator, sauna board calculator
existingToolsThatRank.whatMakesItRank: Niche but functional. Ranks because it exists and no one else has it.
existingToolsThatRank.opportunity: Lower priority — more relevant to DIY builders than pre-built buyers

toolsThatDoNotExistYet.description: Beyond purchase price: estimates installation, electrical upgrade, permits, maintenance, and operating costs over 5 years. Outputs a 'true cost' figure. This is the tool that ends the research phase.
targetKeywords: how much does it really cost to own a sauna
toolsThatDoNotExistYet.emailGateRationale: This output is high value and personalized — email exchange is natural. 'Email me my full cost breakdown' is a compelling CTA.

toolsThatDoNotExistYet.description: 6-8 questions: indoor or outdoor, traditional or infrared, space dimensions, budget, primary use case (recovery/social/daily ritual/health), household size. Outputs: recommended type + top 3 brand matches + estimated cost range.
targetKeywords: which sauna is right for me
what type of sauna should I buy
toolsThatDoNotExistYet.emailGateRationale: Gate results with 'Email me this recommendation' — results page is personalized and worth exchanging an email for.
toolsThatDoNotExistYet.buildComplexity: Medium — can use Typeform embedded or build natively in Next.js
toolsThatDoNotExistYet.seoNote: Make the quiz page a full content page, not a bare /pages/quiz URL. The quiz should live inside editorial content that targets 'which sauna is right for me' and related queries. This is what no competitor has done.

toolsThatDoNotExistYet.description: Input room dimensions and ceiling height, get: minimum clearance requirements, bench layout options, door swing requirements, ventilation requirements. Visual or text output.
targetKeywords: how much space do I need for a home sauna
toolsThatDoNotExistYet.emailGateRationale: Output a printable space plan — 'Email me my space plan' is a natural CTA
toolsThatDoNotExistYet.buildComplexity: Medium to High — visual output adds complexity

priority1.name: The Home Sauna Finder Quiz
priority1.format: Interactive quiz (6-8 questions) with email-gated results
priority1.primaryTargetKeyword: which sauna is right for me
supportingKeywords: what type of sauna should I buy
priority1.whyThisWins: The keyword 'which sauna is right for me' has near-zero competition from content-first brands. Every competitor quiz is buried at /pages/quiz on a Shopify store with no SEO footprint. A full editorial page with an embedded quiz targeting this query cluster will rank and convert simultaneously.
priority1.emailGateMechanic: Show first 2 questions freely, gate results page. Or show results and offer 'Email me this recommendation + a personalized buying checklist' — email adds value without blocking the tool.
priority1.conversionEstimate: 40-50% of completions to email (industry benchmark for interactive tools with personalized output)
priority1.urgency: Competitors are not SEO-targeting this cluster. First-mover advantage available now.

priority2.name: The Home Sauna Buying Checklist PDF
priority2.format: Gated PDF — email exchange for download
supportingKeywords: what to check before buying a sauna
priority2.whyThisWins: SelectSaunas publishes an ungated checklist article. The SafetyCulture sauna checklist is an operational tool (wrong use case). No brand is offering a buyer-stage checklist as a gated lead magnet. The 'sauna buying guide PDF' keyword has explicit download intent with no competition.
checklistContents: Space requirements checklist (room dimensions, ceiling height, ventilation, drainage)
Electrical requirements (dedicated circuit, amperage, panel capacity)
Permit requirements (building permit triggers, electrical permit, setback distances)
Type decision framework (infrared vs traditional vs barrel — 5 qualifying questions)
Installation questions to ask your contractor or supplier
Brand evaluation criteria (warranty, EMF rating, wood quality, customer service)
Hidden costs to budget for (installation, electrical, permits, maintenance)
Questions to ask your partner before committing
priority2.emailGateMechanic: Show table of contents and first section free, gate full PDF. Or: full article page + email gate only for PDF version ('Read online free, download the printable checklist')
priority2.conversionEstimate: 15-25% of page visitors who reach the gate
priority2.effort: Low — 4-6 hours to write and format
priority2.urgency: High — low effort, clear gap, targets explicit download intent

priority3.name: Home Sauna Total Cost-of-Ownership Calculator
priority3.format: Interactive calculator with email-gated full breakdown
supportingKeywords: how much does a home sauna cost
priority3.whyThisWins: beingsauna.com has a cost calculator that ranks but captures no emails. havenofheat.com ranks for cost content but has no interactive tool. A calculator that estimates total cost of ownership (purchase + installation + electrical + permits + 5-year operating cost) is genuinely more useful than any existing tool and has a clear email exchange value: 'Email me my full 5-year cost breakdown.'
calculatorInputs: Sauna type (infrared / traditional electric / wood-fired / pre-built kit / custom build)
Existing electrical panel capacity (upgrade needed Y/N)
outputs: Permit cost estimate for their state
Cost per session at their use frequency
priority3.emailGateMechanic: Show summary numbers free, gate 'Full itemized breakdown + recommended brands at my budget' — email gives them a deeper report
priority3.conversionEstimate: 35-45% of completions to email (personalized financial output is high-value exchange)
priority3.effort: Medium — 1-2 weeks to build in Next.js
priority3.urgency: High — beingsauna.com is ranking for this without email capture. Take the traffic AND the email.

priority4.name: Infrared vs Traditional vs Barrel Comparison PDF
priority4.format: Single-page visual comparison chart — email exchange or free
priority4.whyThisWins: No competitor offers a portable, printable comparison chart. This is the artifact buyers bring to partner conversations and showroom visits. It travels via WhatsApp and Reddit threads.
priority4.formatNotes: One landscape page. 3 columns (infrared / traditional / barrel). Rows: heat type, cost range, installation complexity, electrical requirements, heat-up time, health applications, maintenance, ideal for. sauna.guide branding. URL to configurator at bottom.
priority4.emailGateMechanic: Offer free download in exchange for email. Or: post the low-resolution version on Pinterest and gate the high-res printable version.
priority4.conversionEstimate: 8-15% of visitors who see the offer
priority4.effort: Very Low — 2-3 hours in Canva or Figma
priority4.urgency: Very High — fastest win available, can distribute on Reddit and Pinterest immediately

contentGapsForLeadMagnetPages.gap: No dedicated page targeting 'which sauna is right for me' query
contentGapsForLeadMagnetPages.opportunity: Create /guides/which-home-sauna-is-right-for-me as an editorial page with embedded quiz. This query has explicit tool intent but no content-first brand has built for it.

contentGapsForLeadMagnetPages.gap: No downloadable buying checklist as a lead magnet anywhere in the space
contentGapsForLeadMagnetPages.opportunity: Create /guides/home-sauna-buying-checklist with gated PDF download. The query 'sauna buying guide PDF' has explicit download intent and zero content competition.

contentGapsForLeadMagnetPages.gap: No total cost-of-ownership tool (all cost calculators focus on purchase price only)
contentGapsForLeadMagnetPages.opportunity: The real buyer question is 'what will this actually cost me all-in over 5 years?' No tool answers this. Build it and capture every serious buyer who reaches the cost research phase.

contentGapsForLeadMagnetPages.gap: No permit/installation requirements page structured as a checklist with state-level detail
contentGapsForLeadMagnetPages.opportunity: Multiple competitors have generic installation guides but none go deep on state-by-state permit requirements. A person searching 'home sauna permit requirements' is very close to buying.

contentGapsForLeadMagnetPages.gap: No 'questions to ask before buying a home sauna' resource
contentGapsForLeadMagnetPages.opportunity: Question-based formats match PAA boxes and voice search. 'What questions should I ask when buying a sauna' is an uncontested long-tail. A structured 'Questions to Ask' article with downloadable PDF checklist serves this intent perfectly.

aiSearchOptimization.perplexityBehavior: Perplexity surfaces vendor sites (nordicasauna.com, havenofheat.com), general wellness sources (healthline, webmd), and Reddit threads for sauna buying queries. It does not currently have a strong editorial sauna brand to cite.
howToGetCited: Structure content with explicit, quotable answer paragraphs at the top of each section (Perplexity extracts these)
Add FAQ schema markup to all tool and guide pages
Include specific data points: percentages, price ranges, time estimates — AI cites specifics over generalities
Get featured in Reddit threads on r/Sauna and r/HomeImprovement with links to the configurator — Perplexity cites Reddit at 6.6% of responses
The buying checklist and configurator, once live, will be cited because they are the only tools of their kind from a content-first brand
aiSearchOptimization.chatGPTBehavior: ChatGPT recommends the well-known vendor brands (Sunlighten, Finnleo, Almost Heaven) for product recommendations, and general health sites for benefits. For 'how to choose' queries it recommends checklist-style thinking — exactly what our checklist page would provide.
aiSearchOptimization.contentStructureForAI: Each guide should have: one-sentence direct answer at the top, comparison table where applicable, numbered checklist in the recommendation sections, cited data points. This structure is both user-friendly and AI-extractable.

pillarPageOptimizations.action: Add inline CTA for configurator at the 'which type is right for you' section — this is where decision friction peaks
pillarPageOptimizations.action2: Add 'Complete Buyer's Checklist' download CTA at bottom of page — email exchange for PDF
pillarPageOptimizations.action3: Add FAQ schema for the top 5 questions buyers ask (what does a home sauna cost, infrared vs traditional, permits needed, electrical requirements, how long to install)

pillarPageOptimizations.action: Add cost calculator tool or prominent CTA to cost calculator page
pillarPageOptimizations.action2: This page already exists — add inline CTA after the pricing section for the configurator
pillarPageOptimizations.action3: Internal link to /guides/ultimate-home-sauna-buying-guide as the 'next step after budgeting'

technicalSEO.urlStructureRecommendation: Keep quiz/tools at /guides/ or /tools/ — not /pages/. The /pages/ Shopify convention is dead for SEO. A dedicated editorial URL with keyword-matching slug is what separates sauna.guide from every competitor tool that lives at /pages/sauna-quiz.
schemaMarkup: FAQ schema on all guide pages
HowTo schema on checklist and installation pages
BreadcrumbList on all guide and tool pages

thisWeek.action: Create the Comparison Cheat Sheet PDF (infrared vs traditional vs barrel)
thisWeek.why: Fastest lead magnet to produce. Immediately shareable on Reddit and Pinterest. Serve as inline CTA on existing comparison pages while the quiz is built.

thisWeek.action: Add inline lead magnet CTA to /guides/ultimate-home-sauna-buying-guide and /guides/infrared-vs-traditional-sauna
thisWeek.why: Both pages likely already receiving organic traffic. Adding an email capture CTA is pure upside with no content required.

nextTwoWeeks.why: Low effort, zero competition, targets explicit download intent ('sauna buying guide PDF')
Targets uncontested keyword cluster with the most aligned lead magnet format

month1.why: Most-searched tool in the space. beingsauna.com is ranking but capturing nothing. Take the traffic and the email.
